programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at everything in the kitchen but the sink, with Victoria Wood, Richard Osman, Jason Manford and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Young Jedi Adventures
programme.desc: Kai and Lys seek out a cure for Nubs' illness.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Mavis convinces Pedro and Wendy to each get to know their new step-parents, but things quickly go wrong when Wendy doesn't eat Shonda's patented gross beef.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Wendy takes care of a sick Mavis, but Sophie and Charlotte also come to look after her. Then, Hank and Pedro somehow convince them all it's a competition for Mavis' love!

programme.title: The InBESTigators
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Exciting Excursion
programme.desc: The Grade Five rock climbing excursion is jeopardised when the permission slips go missing, but Maudie suspects they may have been hidden on purpose.

programme.title: The InBESTigators
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Very Lost Notebook
programme.desc: When Maudie loses her beloved notebook, Ezra, Ava and Kyle band together to reunite their friend with her most prized possession.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: After Chloe accuses Marinette of stealing her new bracelet, Hawk Moth akumatizes Chloe's friend's policeman father into the tyrannical Rogercop.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: When Marinette's class makes a short horror film, the lead actress is akumatized into a slimy monster that feeds off fear.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon observe Jump City.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The farmer receives a mystery gift from his antipodean cousin. Unimpressed with the present, he attempts to throw it away only for it to keep reappearing. He becomes convinced it has magical powers.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: The gang meet George Takei in a haunted Hollywood mansion.

programme.title: We Baby Bears
programme.desc: In an endless junkyard the Bears meet Teddi, a sentient teddy bear.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: When Bliss brings a new tech product to town, it's more powerful than imagined.

programme.title: Open Season: Call of Nature
programme.desc: After falling in with a cool group of possums who have turned out to be camp-robbers, Boog must figure out how to rescue Elliot and get all the Campers' stolen belongings back.

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: Hedgehog wants to win the Golden Science Badge but runs into some unexpected competition.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin get caught in the middle of a trash-can feud between Dad and Mr. Robinson.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans want to be the scariest trick-or-treaters in town, so Raven casts a spell that turns them into real monsters.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Accept the Next Proposition You Hear
programme.desc: The Titans start taking their cues from fortune cookies.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: As everything turns upside down in their love lives, Ladybug and Cat Noir must exchange their Miraculous to escape a ruthless villain.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: Unikingdom's throwing a kingdom-wide dance - a fun event where everyone dresses up and busts a move. Everyone's excited... except for Richard, who's terrified to let loose and feel his inner groove.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: Terapagos uses its power to purify Laqua, but a new threat waits in the shadows...

programme.title: Good Game: Spawn Squad
programme.sub-title: Isobel and Gem Rank Teamwork Games!
programme.desc: Join Isobel and Gem as they rank 4 top-tier teamwork games! With Overcooked 2, Unrailed! Moving Out 2 and Unravel Two in the mix, how will they possibly choose which is best!?

programme.title: Let's Dance
programme.desc: Today we're going to learn how to Breakdance.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Uncertain about jumping to the top of the vowels' acrobatic tower, Alphablock U rejects the offer of a crash MAT, a safety NET and new KIT. But, excited to HOP, he goes UP at last.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.sub-title: Fractions are a Piece of Cake
programme.desc: One has been invited to the Fraction Friends party and first meets One Whole. How will the friends share the party food? Luckily they have a song that helps them work it all out!

programme.title: Gardening Australia Junior
programme.desc: Clarence drops by a little country school with a dream of planting a native! It's time to dig, plant and grow. The grand finale? A community celebration bursting with proud junior gardeners!

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: Matt sports a punk like hairstyle, blue face and fancy necklace called a torq - just like the Celts. Paige insists her horned helmet is not a Viking style but Celtic, and shows how to make wool the Celtic way.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: From William The Conqueror making rules about chopping off your dangly bits to Elizabeth I in court over some swan-related mischief, we take a look at some of our Ridiculous Rules.

programme.title: btn
programme.desc: A high-energy, fun way for upper primary and secondary students to learn about current issues and events in their world. Each program is built around major news stories of the week.

programme.title: The Ancient World
programme.desc: Explores the rise and fall of Ancient Egypt, its advancements in farming, construction, mathematics and medicine, and the legacy it left for the world today.

programme.title: BtN Newsbreak
programme.desc: Find out what's going on in the world with a news bulletin designed for children, featuring stories that are relevant and easy to understand.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil visits John and Paul Campbell in Largs in Scotland's central belt, hoping to convince the brothers to clean up their act after letting 11 months go by without finding a buyer for their inherited bungalow.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey wants a tattoo, Nick fancies becoming a dot com millionaire. and Michael tries to prevent his parents from learning that he's being bullied at school.

programme.title: Shetland
programme.desc: After the discovery of an unknown body, the search for Connor grows increasingly desperate. Perez follows Danny on a perilous journey into his murky past.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.desc: Rising tension between Sherlock and Watson bring their partnership to a crossroads, but they endeavor to put their differences aside while they help Mycroft, who faces accusations of treason and murder.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Luka welcomes his longtime friend, Dr Gordana Horvat, to Chicago. A psychotic patient threatens Abby, Romano continues to make rash judgements, Kerry suspects a woman poisoned her own baby, and Carter receives bad news.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Award-winning host Stephen Fry wrangles a stellar panel of comedians in the world's most impossible quiz show. This episode features Alan Davies, John Sessions, Emma Thompson and David Mitchell.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Highlights from the third series, where guests try to dupe their opponents with plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon alongside team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Can man beat machine in a 30-ft, standing-start sprint? Can a driver lose 10 lbs during a race? Can the suction created by a speeding car rip loose a manhole cover during a street race?

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil goes undercover to view an outdated property in Uddingston on the outskirts of Glasgow. He meets Pamela and Ian, whose five-bed has only had 12 viewings in six months.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Bram Vis, wife Lisa and their two children who plan to build a house on an idyllic seaside plot on the Isle of Wight, with generous entertaining spaces and a swimming pool for the family.

programme.title: Claire Hooper's House of Games
programme.desc: Shacking up in Claire Hooper's House of Games this week, we've got ABC's Zan Rowe, footy great Bob Murphy, and comedians Peter Helliar and Geraldine Hickey battling it out for the trophy and a stunning fondue set!

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Katy Steele, Shane Jacobson, John Reid & Mikey Robins
programme.desc: Joining Adam, Myf and Alan are Katy Steele from Little Birdy, John Reid, Shane Jacobson and Mikey Robins.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Join Sandi Toksvig for a look at \.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: David Baddiel, Ed Balls, Jo Brand & Kimberly Wyatt
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by panellists David Baddiel, Ed Balls, Jo Brand, and Kimberly Wyatt.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Julie Hesmondhalgh, Chris McCausland, Yinka Bokinni & Harriet Kemsley
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back with Lee Mack and David Mitchell as team captains. Guests include Julie Hesmondhalgh, Chris McCausland, Yinka Bokinni and Harriet Kemsley.

programme.title: Gruen
programme.desc: Australia's favourite multi-award winning, multi-award losing, longest running, and most importantly, the only show about advertising is coming back for an 18th season.

programme.title: Planet America
programme.desc: Chas and John cover the latest on Iran, the Strait of Hormuz, the ceasefire and peace talks. Plus the growing scandal surrounding FBI chief Kash Patel, and King Charles' visit to mark the 250 years of American independence.

programme.title: Koala Man
programme.desc: Kevin is inspired by Big Greg to write a theme song for Koala Man. But when Kevin forgets to take the rubbish bins down, a monstrous beast hungry for trash is unleashed. With all the rubbish lying around, Vicky gets into DIY.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: While dealing with his own loss, Carter tries to prevent a grieving gang member from seeking revenge. Elsewhere, a baseball player receives treatment for a heart ailment and Susan has trouble ending her marriage.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey wants a tattoo, Nick fancies becoming a dot com millionaire. and Michael tries to prevent his parents from learning that he's being bullied at school.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Count down the hottest chart hits and catch up on some of the most promising new popular music videos on rage.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: The PM's gift from above, our climate solutions are a gas, Perth (briefly) in lockdown, Australian trade in the uncomfortable chair, Nina Oyama under a bridge, and Luke McGregor in aged care. Yes, he looks young for his age.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: Aussie cricketers caught out in India, Australia in the race for space, Andrew Laming too empathetic for his own good, and are we going to war with China? Plus, Frank Woodley has a robot and Dane Simpson has a mouse in his house.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: We roll out the vaccine ourselves, dodge falling rockets, and discuss the winners and losers from the budget. The losers? Anyone who watched it. Zoe Coombs Marr gets in your head, and Nazeem Hussain gets in the spirit of Eid.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: Borders are closed, the Middle East erupts, the royals are on the rocks, and Charlie is on his bike. Plus, Tony Armstrong has terrible news, and Margaret Pomeranz watches terrible television.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The program has now reached the letter R and a rather 'rude' encounter. Joining Sandi Toksvig are John Barrowman, Phill Jupitus, Aisling Bea and regular panellist Alan Davies.

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Award-winning host Stephen Fry wrangles a stellar panel of comedians in the world's most impossible quiz show. This episode features Alan Davies, John Sessions, Emma Thompson and David Mitchell.

programme.title: Young Jedi Adventures
programme.desc: The young Jedi set out to catch a thief.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Uncle Gene's been invited to a performance of the Northern Frights. He doesn't want to go so Mavis tricks him into attending without realising she's leading him to his doom!

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Maggie the ghostwriter is back and she's got a snazzy new red blazer. And she's using that blazer to sell Hotel Transylvania unless Mavis and the gang can stop her in time.

programme.title: The InBESTigators
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Suspect Sprinkler
programme.desc: When the timer on Mrs Maniaci's sprinkler system is tampered with before the Garden of the Year competition, the Inbestigators must track down the culprit.

programme.title: The InBESTigators
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Peculiar Pop Quiz
programme.desc: Maudie and Ava infiltrate Mr Barker's grade six class to watch his famous pop quiz, where Maudie uncovers a crime.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: Cat Noir and Ladybug must put everything else aside to save the day when Kim becomes akumatized as Dark Cupid on Valentine's Day.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: Each story has its beginning. Discover how Marinette and Adrien, two high school students practically like everyone else, became Ladybug and Cat Noir, and why Hawk Moth is their sworn enemy.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy shares his birthday with Starfire.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: Shaggy competes against singer Halsey in a poetry slam.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: When the girls tell Barry he's a hero, he takes it too far.

programme.title: Open Season: Call of Nature
programme.desc: On an unpleasantly hot day, Boog and Elliot grow tired of Ian's pompous criticisms of their leadership style and decide to give him the reigns to Camp Wild to teach him a lesson. What could possibly go wrong?

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: Hedgehog tries to get over a crush so she can regain her focus on magic.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: With love in the air, Gumball is caught in the centre of an emotional hurricane - literally!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Justice League's Next Top Talent Idol Star: Second Greatest Team Edition Part: 1
programme.desc: The Justice League holds another talent competition to find the second best superhero team in the world.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Control Freak informs the Titans that they are on a TV show.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: Ladybug and Cat Noir must face a new enemy - mysterious, worrying, elusive, and yet close to them. Very close, but they have no idea.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: During the gang's Halloween party, Unikitty discovers Rick isn't scared of anything! To get him in the holiday spirit, they each tell their own ideas of what a spooky story is.

programme.title: We Baby Bears
programme.desc: The Bears help Unica return an endangered alien worm to its home.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.sub-title: Eyes to the Skies Once Again(1)
programme.desc: Life's back to normal for Roy and friends until wild Pokemon begin to act strangely...

programme.title: Good Game: Spawn Squad
programme.desc: Four epic challenges, two amazing gamers and one winner! George and Harry go head-to-head in Minecraft, racing through underground, sticky, underwater and griefing mazes!

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Every time the postman pays the Farmer a visit, Bitzer's canine instincts get the better of him, resulting in shredded mail and and an irate farmer.

programme.title: Let's Dance
programme.desc: Dancers will be practicing their Sautes which means to jump in French. They'll be jumping into the finale in a cool, mystical world.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Octonaughty uses his new invention, the Fractionator, to break into The Museum of Numbers to steal the Golden Infinity. But has he met his match when he splits Octoblock into four equal parts?

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock L tells how Alphablock U helps a fox CUB to find its mum. They cross a river in a TUB, shelter in a HUT, fly on the back of a BUG, and find MUM in a forest clearing.

programme.title: Let's Sing!
programme.desc: Jess Hitchcock sings Mother Mountain from the Dhungala Choral Connection Song Book.

programme.title: Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Learn about the orchestra, different timbres and body percussion.

programme.title: The Brilliant World of Tom Gates
programme.desc: Tom and Norman are at Derek's house for band practice; Tom shows how to make a drum kit; Liz draws actual dog zombies.

programme.title: BtN: Media Literacy
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered what makes news news? We look at the news values that determine which stories we see and hear about and which ones we don't, and how those news values can subtly shape the way we see the world.

programme.title: Media Explained: Misinformation
programme.desc: Learn the difference between misinformation and disinformation, how they are created and shared, and the impact they can have on society.

programme.title: Media Stamped
programme.desc: Why do influencers and celebs look so good? And what happens to YOU if you scroll those \.

programme.title: Move It Mobstyle
programme.desc: This program is for everyone; it's about getting off the couch and working up a sweat to make you feel happy and healthy. Darren Compton teams up with Courtney Walter for a special reconciliation dance.

programme.title: BtN Newsbreak
programme.desc: Find out what's going on in the world with a news bulletin designed for children, featuring stories that are relevant and easy to understand.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil goes undercover to view an outdated property in Uddingston on the outskirts of Glasgow. He meets Pamela and Ian, whose five-bed has only had 12 viewings in six months.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben sets Janey up on a blind date with the son of one of his wealthiest clients, Nick decides to become an artist and Susan tries to convince Ben of the merits of opera.

programme.title: Shetland
programme.desc: Mounting evidence pushes Perez and the team to revise their view of Connor Cairns. Is he a vulnerable missing person or a killer on the run?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It's the team's most confounding puzzle yet when a passenger seemingly vanishes mid-flight to Dominica, only to be found dead in Saint Marie. Meanwhile, an old face returns and Neville has a huge decision to make.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: While dealing with his own loss, Carter tries to prevent a grieving gang member from seeking revenge. Elsewhere, a baseball player receives treatment for a heart ailment and Susan has trouble ending her marriage.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry and the panel look at all manner of 'Hoaxes'. Joining the QI Master are Alan Davies, Danny Baker, Sean Lock and David Mitchell.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse, Fred Sirieix
programme.desc: The guests joining Lee Mack and David Mitchell are James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Can concentrated hydrofluoric acid in a bathtub completely dissolve a human body, create a hole through the tub, and dissolve the bathroom floor below it?

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Neil and Jackie Harris are desperate for more space for them and their two boys. But Phil discovers a house with a spiral stairway hiding mounds of clutter, a garden full of rubble and a bathroom from the past.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Angelo Mastropietro who wants to become a 21st century British caveman. How does one make a cave comfortable for contemporary living? It's a unique challenge that turns into an obsession.

programme.title: Claire Hooper's House of Games
programme.desc: Shacking up in Claire Hooper's House of Games this week, we've got ABC's Zan Rowe, footy great Bob Murphy, and comedians Peter Helliar and Geraldine Hickey battling it out for the trophy and a stunning fondue set!

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Richard Gill, George McEncroe, Laurie Cadevida & Jono Coleman
programme.desc: Joining Adam Hills are Richard Gill, Laurie Cadevida, Jono Coleman and George McEncroe.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry goes decidedly kitsch with Sue Perkins, Reginald D Hunter, Jimmy Carr and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Shirley Ballas, Steven Bartlett, Josie Gibson and Chris McCausland
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell as team captains.

programme.title: The Truman Show
programme.desc: Truman doesn't realise that his quaint hometown is a giant studio set run by a visionary director, that folks living and working there are Hollywood actors, and that even his incessantly bubbly wife is a contract player.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Pratt and Carter alternately treat a woman whose unborn child was brutally cut out of her, victims from a mass cult poisoning, a wounded gang member, a dying Buddhist nun, and a heart attack victim.

programme.title: Like a Version
programme.desc: triple j's legendary Like A Version segment hosts the greatest local and international acts, performing a cover of a song they love, and one of their own.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Anthony Fantano guest programmed rage in 2014. A decade on, we're feeling a strong 6 to light 7 for this one.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben sets Janey up on a blind date with the son of one of his wealthiest clients, Nick decides to become an artist and Susan tries to convince Ben of the merits of opera.

programme.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy
programme.desc: Enter the world of 1970s TV news, where Ron Burgundy and his news team are the top rated news source. All is well in their male-dominated world of news until rising star reporter Veronica Corningstone turns it upside down.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Tom Allen, Rosie Jones and Kae Kurd
programme.desc: The inimitable Tom Allen introduces the fantastic Rosie Jones and the wonderful Kae Kurd to the world-famous Apollo stage.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry and the panel look at all manner of 'Hoaxes'. Joining the QI Master are Alan Davies, Danny Baker, Sean Lock and David Mitchell.

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig embarks on the first part of her 'quest' for knowledge with Alan Davies, Alan Carr, Alice Levine, and Phill Jupitus.

programme.title: Young Jedi Adventures
programme.sub-title: Lys and The Snowy Mountain Rescue
programme.desc: The younglings help wrangle creatures.
